questions.question: Explain why the degree distribution in random networks follows a Poisson distribution.
expectedPoints: Each edge exists independently with probability p
Degree of a node follows binomial distribution B(n-1, p)
For large n and small p, binomial approximates Poisson distribution
Poisson parameter λ = p(n-1) represents average degree

questions.question: Discuss the limitations of the random network model in capturing properties of real-world networks, and suggest two alternative models that address these limitations.
expectedPoints: Random networks have Poisson degree distribution while many real networks have power-law distribution
Random networks have low clustering (C ≈ p) while real networks often have high clustering
Watts-Strogatz model addresses high clustering through rewiring
Barabási-Albert model addresses scale-free property through preferential attachment
Random networks lack community structure found in real networks
Random networks serve as important null models despite limitations
